title: Calculus Foundations
description: Single-variable calculus for ML practitioners who know algebra and trigonometry. Covers the derivative as a limit, differentiation rules (power, product, quotient, chain), exponentials and logarithms, optimization and gradient descent, and integration with the Fundamental Theorem. Every concept is anchored to its role in training neural networks.

readings.title: Derivatives: Rate of Change and the Limit
readings.description: The difference quotient, the limit definition of the derivative, the power rule, and an introduction to partial derivatives as gradients in ML.

readings.title: Differentiation Rules
readings.description: Constant, sum, product, and quotient rules; derivatives of sin and cos; higher derivatives and concavity; L2 regularization and MSE gradients.

readings.title: The Chain Rule
readings.description: Composed functions, the chain rule in prime and Leibniz notation, chains of three or more functions, and the direct connection to backpropagation.

readings.title: Exponentials, Logarithms, and Their Derivatives
readings.description: The natural exponential e^x and its self-derivative, ln x, chain-rule combinations, derivation of the sigmoid derivative σ(x)(1−σ(x)), and cross-entropy loss.

readings.title: Optimization and Linear Approximation
readings.description: Critical points, the second derivative test, local vs global minima, the first-order Taylor approximation, and gradient descent derived from the linear approximation.

readings.title: Integration and the Fundamental Theorem
readings.description: Area under a curve, Riemann sums, antiderivatives, the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, key antiderivative rules, and integration in ML: PDFs, expected values, entropy, KL divergence.

quizzes.title: Derivatives and Differentiation Rules
quizzes.description: 8 questions covering the limit definition, power rule, product rule, chain rule, sigmoid derivative, log derivative, and backpropagation as chain rule application.

quizzes.title: Optimization, Linear Approximation, and Integration
quizzes.description: 8 questions covering critical points, the second derivative test, linear approximation, gradient descent updates, the Fundamental Theorem, PDF normalization, expected value as integral, and a gradient step implementation.

labs.title: Derivatives in Practice
labs.description: Implement finite differences, verify differentiation rules numerically, visualize the secant-to-tangent convergence, and explore the sigmoid derivative with PyTorch autograd.

labs.title: Gradient Descent from Scratch
labs.description: Implement gradient descent for 1D and 2D functions, run learning rate experiments, fit a linear regression by hand, and confirm the result matches torch.optim.SGD.

labs.title: Integration and Probability
labs.description: Build a trapezoidal integrator, verify the Fundamental Theorem, confirm Gaussian normalization and the 68–95–99.7 rule, estimate π via Monte Carlo, and compute KL divergence numerically.
